frothy wipe-user — clear user-tier definitions on a running device; library tier survives

Wipe-user clears the user-tier definitions on a running device, leaving the library tier and the firmware in place. Use it when you want to start with a clean slate of your own definitions without touching library code the project installed. It opens the serial port and asks the device to drop its user overlay.

Examples:
  frothy wipe-user --port /dev/cu.usbserial-0001
      clear user-tier definitions on the board on that port

Flags:
  -port string
    	serial port, for example /dev/cu.usbserial-0001
